mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 20-day SPX returns fails to exceed 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 5.0%, the labor market is sufficiently weak to signal an impending recession, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In at least 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is negative; otherwise the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous elevation of the federal funds rate and the unemployment rate creates a stagflationary environment where traditional valuation drivers break down, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to heightened uncertainty and discount rate compression.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS * UNRATE) in a predictive regression of 5-day forward SPX returns does not yield a negative coefficient significant at the 5% level over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: During the late-cycle expansion regime (defined by low unemployment and high industrial production), a decline in housing starts (HOUST) is an early warning of peaking residential investment, which systematically precedes equity drawdowns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When the regime condition holds, month-over-month declines in HOUST are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in fewer than 60% of cases over the past 15 years.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below the 100-index threshold signals industrial contraction and pending economic weakness, which erodes corporate earnings and depresses equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After INDPRO falls below 100, the forward 20-day SPX return is negative in less than 55% of occurrences over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate strengthening labor markets, which boost consumer confidence and disposable income, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 20-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPI > 300), further increases in the CPI index amplify expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses valuation multiples and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, the rank correlation between month-over-month CPI change and forward 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p < 0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1400 thousand units, it signals a downturn in the housing sector that leads to reduced construction employment, lower consumer wealth, and broader economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After HOUST falls below 1400, the 60-day forward SPX return is negative in fewer than 55% of instances over a 15-year period.
